title: OnsiteGene 4 Protocol for Saliva Sample Collection
author: yliu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bpjemkje
materials: Ensure all collection materials are labelled with the correct identifying information.
The total volume measured should exclude any bubbles.

steps.title: Saliva sample should be collected with...
steps.instruction: Saliva sample should be collected with the assistance of a healthcare worker or technician.

steps.title: Before collection, clean hands using alcohol-based...
steps.instruction: Before collection, clean hands using alcohol-based sanitizer or soap and water (no fragrances) and wear appropriate PPE (at minimum, gloves and a mask).

steps.title: Back away as far as possible...
steps.instruction: Back away as far as possible from the sample donor during the whole process. While preparing collection materials, direct the sample donor to begin pooling saliva in their mouth.

steps.title: This protocol is intended for the...
steps.instruction: This protocol is intended for the collection of the normal saliva that naturally pools into the mouth. No coughing or sniffing prior to sample collection is required. Ideally, water should be avoided 10 minutes prior to collection. Other drinks, food, and nasal sprays should be avoided half an hour before sample collection.

steps.title: Open the lid of the saliva...
steps.instruction: Open the lid of the saliva collection tube, and direct the sample provider to gently expel saliva into the container until at least 500 μL is collected. Securely replace the lid of the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 μL of OnsiteGene storage...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of OnsiteGene storage media to each sample tube and secure the lid of each tube.

steps.title: Sterilize the tube surface with 70%...
steps.instruction: Sterilize the tube surface with 70% ethanol or a disinfecting wipe, and place the sample in a secondary container or an appropriately labeled biohazard bag.

steps.title: Dispose of gloves, and register the...
steps.instruction: Dispose of gloves, and register the sample collection information (including date and time).

steps.title: Transfer the sample at room temperature for sample processing
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample at room temperature for sample processing. The virus RNA remains stable at room temperature for 3-5 days.

steps.title: Vortex each sample collection tube for...
steps.instruction: Vortex each sample collection tube for 5 seconds at 3000-5000 RPM.

steps.title: This step can be substituted by...
steps.instruction: This step can be substituted by tapping or shaking each sample collection tube to mix the sample.

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 15 seconds using centrifuge
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 15 seconds using centrifuge.

steps.title: The purpose is to
steps.instruction: The purpose is to: 1) Remove liquid from the cap to avoid the contamination when cap is removed; 2) Spin down solid particles to the bottom of the tube and allow supernatant to be acquired.

steps.title: Transfer 900 µL supernatant of the...
steps.instruction: Transfer 900 µL supernatant of the sample into the cartridge of magnetic beads RNA extraction instrument from OnsiteGene.

steps.title: Load the cartridge into the extraction...
steps.instruction: Load the cartridge into the extraction instrument to extract and purify the viral RNA. The duration of the process is 6 minutes without any hand-on work. The sample elution volume is 50 µL after the extraction. The extraction instrument can process 32 samples in one run.

steps.title: Transfer 7
steps.instruction: Transfer 7.5 µL extracted RNA sample (this sample can be stored at -20°C for 14 days) from the cartridge and 7.5 µL OnsiteGene Hi-Sense TM COVID-19 detection kit reaction mix (with primers and probes) into a superfast RT-qPCR reaction tube. Secure the lid of the tube.

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 15...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 15 seconds using centrifuge or spinner.

steps.title: This step can be substituted by...
steps.instruction: This step can be substituted by hand-shaking the tube to get the samples to the bottom.

steps.title: Load up to 16 superfast RT-qPCR...
steps.instruction: Load up to 16 superfast RT-qPCR reaction tubes onto the OnsiteGene XDive Superfast Real-Time PCT instrument and run the following conditions: %s %s Step Temperature Time 1 50°C 30 sec 2 95°C 0 sec 3 60°C 2 sec 4 Read Fluorescence intensity and overhead (10 sec) 5 Repeat steps 2-4 for 40 cycles.

steps.title: failure to detect sufficient specimen volume
steps.instruction: failure to detect sufficient specimen volume. The sample needs to be retested. * In the case of one SARS-Cov-2 target positive (CT ≤

steps.title: and one SARS-Cov-2 target negative (CT...
steps.instruction: and one SARS-Cov-2 target negative (CT > 40), the result is suggestive of:
